prompt: Which driver dominated the 2023 Formula 1 season by winning a record-breaking 19 races and scoring 575 points?
explanation: Max Verstappen had a historic 2023 season with Red Bull, capturing 19 wins and 21 podiums.

prompt: Who won the 2025 Formula 1 World Championship driving for McLaren with a Mercedes engine?
explanation: According to the table, Lando Norris won the 2025 championship at age 26, taking 7 wins and 18 podiums.

prompt: Which team chassis did Michael Schumacher drive when he won his first two World Championships in 1994 and 1995?
explanation: Michael Schumacher won his first two titles driving for Benetton before moving to Ferrari.

prompt: How many race victories did Nigel Mansell secure during his dominant 1992 championship year with Williams?
explanation: Nigel Mansell won 9 races and captured an impressive 14 pole positions during the 1992 season.

prompt: Which tyre supplier provided the rubber for Fernando Alonso's championship-winning Renault cars in 2005 and 2006?
explanation: Renault and Fernando Alonso used Michelin (M) tyres to secure their consecutive titles.

prompt: Who won the 2009 World Championship driving for the fairytale debut team Brawn GP?
explanation: Jenson Button won the 2009 title with Brawn GP, securing 6 victories along the way.

prompt: Which engine manufacturer powered Sebastian Vettel's Red Bull racing cars to four consecutive titles from 2010 to 2013?
explanation: Sebastian Vettel's Red Bull chassis was powered by Renault engines during his entire championship streak.

prompt: At what age did Lewis Hamilton win his very first Formula 1 World Championship in 2008 with McLaren?
explanation: Lewis Hamilton became one of the youngest champions ever in 2008 when he won the title at age 23.

prompt: Which driver won the 2016 World Championship with Mercedes and immediately retired after defeating Lewis Hamilton?
explanation: Nico Rosberg won 9 races and scored 385 points to narrowly beat Hamilton for the 2016 crown.

prompt: How many pole positions did Ayrton Senna achieve during his 1990 championship-winning campaign with McLaren?
explanation: Ayrton Senna took 10 pole positions out of the season on his way to the 1990 world title.

statement: Michael Schumacher achieved a podium finish in every single race during the 2002 season, recording 17 podiums in total.
explanation: In 2002, Michael Schumacher put on a display of ultimate consistency, finishing on the podium in all 17 races.

statement: Damon Hill won the 1996 World Championship driving for McLaren-Mercedes.
explanation: Damon Hill won his 1996 title driving for Williams with a Renault engine and Goodyear tyres.

statement: Mika Häkkinen won back-to-back championships in 1998 and 1999 utilizing Bridgestone (B) tyres.
explanation: Mika Häkkinen and McLaren won consecutive titles using Bridgestone tyres during both seasons.

statement: Kimi Räikkönen won the 2007 World Championship for Ferrari by scoring exactly 110 points.
explanation: Kimi Räikkönen secured his only world title in 2007 with 6 wins, 12 podiums, and 110 points.

statement: Max Verstappen scored half-points in the 2021 season, finishing with a total of 395.5 points.
explanation: The dramatic 2021 season ended with Max Verstappen scoring 395.5 points to take his first world title.

statement: Alain Prost won the 1993 Formula 1 World Championship driving for the Benetton team.
explanation: Alain Prost won the 1993 championship driving for Williams-Renault before retiring from the sport.

statement: Jacques Villeneuve won the 1997 title at the age of 26 while securing 7 race wins.
explanation: Jacques Villeneuve took the 1997 crown at age 26 with Williams, registering 7 wins and 10 pole positions.

prompt: Order these Formula 1 World Champions chronologically by their winning year (earliest first).
explanation: Order: Nigel Mansell (1992), Mika Häkkinen (1998), Fernando Alonso (2005), Jenson Button (2009).

explanation: Order: Goodyear (1990), Bridgestone (1998), Michelin (2005), Pirelli (2011).
